DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Van C. Durrer II, Esq. of Skadden, T:213-687-5200, with Jeffrey Goldenhersh, Esq., for defendant/moving party Hosain Rahman;
Gerard M. Mooney, Esq. of Rutan & Tucker LLP, T:714.338.1857, for plaintiff/opposing party Protempo Limited;
Brian Murray, Esq. of Loeb & Loeb LLP, T:310.282.2235 for defendant Jason E. Child, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HOSAIN RAHMAN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Hosain Rahman's demurrer to the first, second, third and thirteenth causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plainitff Protempo Limited is overruled as to the first, second, and third causes of action for promissory fraud, fraud and intentional deceit, and negligent misrepresentation and sustained without leave to amend as to the thirteenth cause of action for violation of Penal Code 496(a). The first, second and third causes of action are alleged with adequate specificity and the allegations of reliance are not necessarily contradicted by the contract documents. A person who commits fraud is liable for doing so even if done as agent for a principal who entered into a contract for which the agent is not a party. The negligent misrepresentaion claim does not allege a nonactionable "negligent false promise," but rather an actionable negligent misrepresentation of existing facts. The Penal Code 496(a) claim fails because Protempo has not alleged, nor does it appear that it can allege, that it transferred money to Mr. Rahman.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

Off Calendar Oct-11-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bryan Murray, Esq. of Loeb & Loeb LLP, Tel. 310.282.2235, for defendant and moving party (joinder) Jason E. Child, by Court Call;
Van C. Durrer, II, Esq. and Jeffrey Goldenhersh, Esq. of Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom LLP, Tel. 213.687.5200, for defendant and moving party Hosain Rahman;
Richard K. Howell, Esq. of Rutan & Tucker LLP, Tel. 714.662.4624, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JASON CHILD'S JOINDER IN DEMURRER FILED BY HOSAIN RAHMAN as follows: Defendant Jason Child's joinder in defendant Hosain Rahman's demurrer to the first and second causes of action for fraud/intentional deceit and for negligent misrepresentaion in the complaint filed by plaintiff Protempo Limited is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both causes of action. Protempo does not sufficiently allege the misrepresentation(s) and/or concealment of fact(s) to satisfy the fraud pleading particularity requirements. In additional to actual and justifiable reliance reliance, Protempo must allege when the actionable conduct occurred, who committed the actionable conduct and by what means and, if the actionable conduct is a concealment of fact, why the concealed fact was required to be disclosed.

Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org.
